      "title": "Mutable default argument in dataclass field",
      "description": "The `queued` field uses `timezone.now()` as a default, which is evaluated at class definition time, not at instance creation time. This means all AssignmentSource instances created without an explicit `queued` argument will share the same timestamp instead of each getting the current time. For cycle prevention logic, this could cause timing-based issues or incorrect comparisons.",
      "suggestion": "Use `field(default_factory=timezone.now)` instead: `queued: datetime = field(default_factory=timezone.now)`"

      "title": "Datetime serialization gap in dict conversion",
      "description": "The `from_dict()` method uses `asdict()` to reconstruct AssignmentSource from a dictionary (line 31), but `asdict()` returns the datetime object as-is. When this dictionary is passed through celery (sync_assignee_outbound task in line 61 of sync.py), datetime objects cannot be serialized by the default JSON encoder. The task will fail to deserialize the `assignment_source_dict` parameter.",
      "suggestion": "Convert datetime to ISO format string in `to_dict()` and parse it back in `from_dict()`. Or ensure the task uses a serializer that supports datetime objects (e.g., pickle)."
